text.title: Rust Project Structure Rules
text.summary: Rules for the layered module structure, AppState design, error centralization, and module visibility in Rust HTTP services.

rule.statement: Organize a Rust HTTP service into: `routes/` (URL wiring), `handlers/` (HTTP parse + response), `services/` (business logic), `db/` (queries), `models/` (domain types), `middleware/`, `config.rs`, `error.rs`.
rule.rationale: Each layer has a single responsibility. This separation allows handler tests without a running DB and service tests without an HTTP runtime.

rule.statement: After creating any file under `routes/` or `handlers/`, immediately add `pub mod <module>;` to the corresponding `mod.rs`. Run `cargo check` after every new file creation.
rule.rationale: The most common LLM mistake: creating `routes/users.rs` but forgetting `pub mod users;` in `routes/mod.rs`, causing a silent compile failure.

rule.statement: Avoid `pub` on everything. Expose only what external layers need; keep internal helpers `pub(crate)` or private.
rule.rationale: Over-broad visibility leaks internal contracts, making refactors break callers that should never have had access.

rule.statement: `AppState` must be a small `Clone` struct of `Arc<T>` fields. Never `Arc<AppState>` wrapping the whole thing — Axum's `State` extractor needs `AppState: Clone` directly.
rule.rationale: `Arc<AppState>` prevents Axum from extracting the state; you get a cryptic `Clone` bound error instead of a clear type error.

rule.statement: Use `Arc<sqlx::PgPool>` for the DB pool in `AppState` — `PgPool` is already safe to share; no extra `Mutex` needed.
rule.rationale: Wrapping `PgPool` in `Mutex` serializes all database access. `PgPool` manages its own connection pool safely.

antipattern.reason: `Rc` and `RefCell` are not `Send + Sync`, violating Axum's state bounds. `Box<dyn Trait>` without explicit `Send + Sync` has the same problem.

rule.statement: Define one `AppError` enum in `error.rs` using `thiserror`. Implement `IntoResponse` once mapping each variant to an HTTP status. Use `From` impls to convert library errors (sqlx, validator) into `AppError`.
rule.rationale: Multiple error types per module cause `match` explosion in handlers. Centralizing in `AppError` ensures every error has a defined HTTP status code.

rule.statement: Prefer `?` with `From` impls (via `thiserror`) over manual error mapping in handlers and services.
rule.rationale: Manual error mapping in every function duplicates the mapping logic and introduces inconsistencies in HTTP status codes.
